what_broke: Pinecone indexing failures caused calls to not be properly processed for customers. Multiple organizations affected, requiring manual re-runs of failed meetings across many customer orgs (Guru, Fleet, Chowly, Syncari, 1Password, Wandb, Spare, Relay, Order, JustWorks, Robin, Snorkel, JellyFish, LiveFlow, Veriff, Alation, Alchemy, and others).
root_cause: Pinecone indexing failures (specific root cause not detailed in channel messages, but resulted in calls not getting processed). Marked as P2 with freshstatus incident page created.
severity_detail: P2 (multiple orgs affected but calls could be re-run; no permanent data loss)
still_relevant: mitigated - all failed meetings were manually re-run and resolved; Pinecone infrastructure dependency remains

what_broke: Zscaler sales calls were incorrectly mapping to Salesforce Account/Opportunity because the Salesforce Event matching was being applied to all departments instead of only GCS (support) departments.
root_cause: The meeting import Salesforce linking algorithm did not filter by host department, causing sales calls to be matched using Salesforce Events (intended only for GCS/support calls). The fix was non-trivial because the Salesforce linking runs before host determination.
still_relevant: mitigated — fix deployed with department-based filtering setting; affected calls were reimported

what_broke: Gong calls were not being mapped/associated to Salesforce opportunities. Call notifications were missing opportunity links for multiple customers.
root_cause: The Salesforce ID association from external attendees in Gong calls was not working correctly. A code change was needed to add in-house SF association even in the trigger payload (PR #2302, #2303, #2304).
still_relevant: mitigated - fixed via PRs, reran affected calls for customers, channel archived Feb 2024

what_broke: Call notifications were not firing for multiple customers. Gong calls were failing to process, and Salesforce IDs were not being copied from attendees (regression).
root_cause: A regression caused Salesforce IDs to not be copied from attendees, breaking call-to-opportunity association. Null attendee names also caused failures. Workflows needed migration to updated format. (PR #2353 - "fix: regression with salesforce ids not copied from attendees")
still_relevant: mitigated - fixed via code changes and manual rerunning of calls for affected customers, channel archived June 2023

what_broke: Closed-won notifications (deal assist feature) were failing. The issue was described as "more of a bug" related to workflow execution with null field labels.
root_cause: A null `fieldLabel` in changelist triggers was causing workflow failures. Related to OneGraph integration returning null values. Fix involved adding logging and fixing label handling (PR #419, #420).
still_relevant: mitigated - fixed in Aug 2021, channel archived Nov 2021

what_broke: SalesforceObjectCreator and SalesforceObjectUpdater workflow actions were failing for multiple customers (Guru, Chowly, Honeycomb). Objects could not be created or updated in Salesforce.
root_cause: Missing Salesforce object metadata caused OneGraph GraphQL queries to be rendered incorrectly (field values were sent without quotes). The system did not fetch metadata for all object types and silently fell back to a quoteless default renderer. Additionally, the frontend was sending field names with incorrect casing (e.g., `credit_fields_Approval_Status__c` instead of `Credit_fields_Approval_Status__c`).
still_relevant: mitigated - fixed via PRs #2009 (fetch metadata on the fly) and #2010 (throw error if metadata not found), channel archived June 2023

what_broke: An external user (from Zscaler/customer org) was incorrectly synced into Momentum's internal org (org 1) via Slack Connect. This caused the user to authenticate with Salesforce under the wrong organization, creating cross-org contamination including dealrooms being created in the wrong org.
root_cause: The Slack user sync job did not filter out external users (users with a different `slackTeamId`). When a customer joined a Slack Connect channel, their user was synced into org 1, and they were able to authenticate with Salesforce under the wrong org.
still_relevant: mitigated - fixed by filtering users by `slackTeamId` in sync job, manual cleanup of affected records, channel archived Aug 2022

what_broke: All Gong API requests were failing with 401 Unauthorized errors across all customers. Gong call syncing and notifications completely stopped working for ~24+ hours.
root_cause: Two compounding bugs during Gong token migration from OneGraph: (1) Auth errors from OneGraph were nested under `response.data.response.errors` instead of `response.data.errors`, so token refresh failures were not detected and no alerts fired. (2) The refresh token was not being persisted after token refresh -- only the first refresh after import worked, subsequent refreshes failed because the old (now-invalid) refresh token was being reused.
still_relevant: mitigated - fixed by persisting refresh tokens on refresh (PR #2244), extended token expiry from 24h to 30 days, all customers had to re-authenticate, channel archived June 2023

what_broke: Hopin's Salesforce API rate limit was being exceeded (>300k requests), triggered by a Honeycomb alert. OneGraph webhook payloads were exceeding the 100KB body size limit, causing 413 errors and triggering re-delivery of events.
root_cause: The request body size limit was set too low (100KB) for webhook payloads from OneGraph/Salesforce subscriptions. Large payloads were rejected, causing OneGraph to retry delivery, which further inflated API request counts against Hopin's SFDC rate limit.
still_relevant: no - Hopin is no longer a customer and the architecture has changed (OneGraph dependency removed).

what_broke: Multiple customer organizations were missing Gong/Wingman call recordings for their meetings. Calls were not being associated with meetings in the system.
root_cause: The Gong call sync workflow was failing to match calls to meetings for several customer accounts (Fleet, Simon Data, Alation, Jasper, 1P, Radar, Screencloud, Carto). The team ran remediation workflows to backfill missing call data.
still_relevant: UNKNOWN

what_broke: The Momentum web application (app.momentum.io) was completely unreachable. Users could not access the site.
root_cause: Netlify had a complete stack outage affecting all sites hosted on their platform. The Momentum frontend was hosted on Netlify, so the app went down with Netlify's infrastructure.
still_relevant: UNKNOWN - depends on whether frontend is still hosted on Netlify.

what_broke: Twingate customer was receiving duplicate/repeated playbook messages in their deal room Slack channels. The bot was posting the same messages multiple times across customer deal channels.
root_cause: A polling-based feature was causing repeated message delivery. The team deleted duplicate bot messages across affected channels and disabled the usePolling feature. A postmortem was written and shared with Twingate.
still_relevant: mitigated - usePolling feature was disabled entirely as a remediation action.

what_broke: User mapping (email/Salesforce ID/Asana ID to Slack user ID) was returning stale data because some Redis cache keys had their TTL set to -1 (never expire), causing incorrect user mappings in workflows. Support console also went down as a side effect.
root_cause: An edge case bug in the `Cacheable` library caused some Redis cache entries to be stored without a TTL (set to -1/infinite) despite the code specifying `ttlSeconds: 120`. This was a known flaky behavior in the Cacheable package that occurred infrequently and without a clear pattern.
still_relevant: mitigated - Immediate fix was flushing the Redis cache; long-term fix discussed was switching to another caching package or adding nightly cache flushes.

what_broke: Slack homescreen (opportunities screen) failed to load for users with Google Calendar integration. The app home tab was completely broken for affected users for ~3 days (Nov 19-22, 2021).
root_cause: New manager opportunity management feature reused the same Slack Action ID ("manage_opportunity") for both manager and non-manager users. Slack Homescreen has a limitation where blocks cannot share the same Action ID, causing a clash when loading.
still_relevant: mitigated - The offending code was reverted and later re-implemented with a different action_id.

what_broke: Deal room creation was extremely slow or timing out. Slack events endpoint had p95 latency of 5.2 seconds (some requests taking 11s+), causing Slack to give up waiting for ack (3s limit). Some requests hit 30s server timeout resulting in 504s.
root_cause: Performance degradation likely caused by blocking code in the event processing path, combined with high latency on GCP PubSub publish calls (9+ seconds). Also found stale OneGraph subscriptions generating noise. The team deployed optimizations including removing an await on the 'dealroom_created' trigger.
still_relevant: mitigated - Performance optimizations were deployed and monitoring improvements (5xx alerts) were planned.

what_broke: GCP PubSub message publishing from the event-receiver service started failing intermittently. Messages from OneGraph (Salesforce event relay) were being rejected by PubSub with "Invalid schema message" / "Message failed schema validation" errors and total timeout errors exceeding 5000ms.
root_cause: GCP PubSub began rejecting Avro-encoded messages that had previously worked fine. Contributing factors included: (1) null event payloads from invalid Salesforce auth causing unexpected failures in batched publishes, (2) PubSub ordering key behavior where a single failed publish blocks all subsequent messages on that key until resumePublishing is called, and (3) unnecessary DB transactions wrapping single UPDATE queries adding latency.
still_relevant: mitigated - Multiple fixes deployed: resumePublishing on ordering key failures, removed unnecessary DB transactions, added custom TypeORM logger for correlation, null event payload handling.

what_broke: Slack events processing became extremely slow due to database lock contention. The `/slack/events` endpoint was timing out, causing Slack to retry requests in a loop. Workflows and cue notifications were delayed or failing.
root_cause: A customer (Twingate) triggered 4400+ TASK_CREATED events in a short time (likely from an automated script), which overwhelmed the workflow engine. Each workflow run triggered multiple DB transactions (TypeORM was wrapping single UPDATE queries in unnecessary transactions), causing heavyweight lock contention on the `assist_definition` table in PostgreSQL. The DB got stuck in lock wait state.
still_relevant: mitigated - Workflow conditions were moved to top-level to avoid unnecessary DB writes, TypeORM transaction isolation was investigated, and slow query logging was added.

what_broke: Subscription event processing (Salesforce events) started failing across all customers. Workflows and notifications stopped triggering.
root_cause: A code refactoring to optimize queries removed some unused relations, but inadvertently assigned `botuser.organization` (which had no organization attached) instead of `context.organization`. This caused all subscription processing to fail. After rolling back the deploy, the issue persisted because `getBotUser` results were cached in Redis with empty `organization` fields. Had to manually flush Redis cache keys to resolve.
still_relevant: mitigated - Fix deployed, Redis cache cleared, RCA report created. Lessons learned about cache invalidation on rollback and need for CircleCI-based rollback mechanism.

what_broke: Cue notifications were not triggering for customers (Radar, Honeycomb) when cue conditions contained custom Salesforce fields.
root_cause: UNKNOWN from available messages (channel was created retroactively for postmortem purposes). The summary indicates issues with cues conditions containing custom fields. A postmortem report was created on Notion.
still_relevant: mitigated - Fix was deployed and moved to monitoring status.

what_broke: 21-minute full outage on April 1, 2022 (1:15 PM - 1:36 PM PST). Incoming Salesforce events were delayed, Slack interactions failed or were unresponsive, and login would have failed.
root_cause: A database deployment (feed payload table persistence) overloaded the system. The deployment caused DB connection exhaustion and lock contention, blocking all queries including on the assist_definition table. The NodeJS event loop was starved. Missing database index on organizationId/triggerType queries and unnecessary DB transactions on feed payload inserts amplified the impact.
still_relevant: mitigated - Rolled back immediately. Action items created: add missing DB index, remove unnecessary transactions, add pubsub subscription throttling.

what_broke: Approval actions and other Slack interactions were not working unless the user had explicitly authenticated. The Narvar customer was affected.
root_cause: A code change added authentication wrappers to Slack action handlers (originally for Deal Assist which requires auth), but this inadvertently required auth for all actions including approvals that should work without explicit user authentication.
still_relevant: mitigated - Quick fix deployed to remove unnecessary auth requirement from approval actions, with a longer-term fix planned to properly scope which actions need auth.

what_broke: Database timeouts were occurring, causing service disruptions. Multiple PRs were needed to fix performance issues.
root_cause: Database queries were timing out due to performance issues; fixes involved multiple PRs to optimize queries and speed up operations (PRs #1148, #1149, #1151).
still_relevant: mitigated - fixed via multiple PRs optimizing database queries, channel archived June 2022

what_broke: Momentum stopped receiving Salesforce events from OneGraph between 11:33 AM and 12:50 PM PT. OneGraph was getting timeouts connecting to api.momentum.io. Auth0 also experienced a service degradation in the US-3 region during the same window.
root_cause: AWS had an internet connectivity issue in the US-EAST-2 region, causing requests from OneGraph (hosted on AWS) to Momentum's GCP static IP to time out. The team mitigated by adding a new IP to the load balancer and updating DNS.
still_relevant: mitigated - OneGraph dependency has been removed, but AWS networking issues could still affect other integrations.

what_broke: Gong users were being marked as unauthenticated. When refreshing OneGraph tokens (via scheduled job or manually), the token came back excluding GONG from loggedInServices, causing users to be marked unauthenticated.
root_cause: Gong (external dependency managed through OneGraph) changed how they respond to the auth check request. The auth flow itself was working, but the loggedInServices response stopped including GONG. OneGraph fixed the issue on their side.
still_relevant: mitigated - Gong auth was migrated away from OneGraph to in-house (ENG-3733 referenced in thread).

what_broke: Multiple customers had their Salesforce users incorrectly marked as unauthenticated. The user.salesforceUserId field was being set to a mismatched value, causing Salesforce integration to break for affected users across multiple orgs (Alation, Chowly, Syncari, Postman, Guru, Owner, Unit21).
root_cause: When a second user in the same org authenticated with Salesforce, the system was overwriting the salesforceUserId on the first user's record with a mismatched foreign user ID. The user_integration.foreignUserId was correct but user.salesforceUserId was wrong. Root cause of the mismatch was not definitively identified; manual DB fixes were applied.
still_relevant: UNKNOWN - no definitive root cause was identified, only manual fixes applied.

what_broke: Significant drop in Zoom meeting events being received and processed after rolling out a new Zoom events endpoint using pub/sub. Customers (Rebuy, Austin's org, others) were missing call analyses because Zoom was not sending all webhook events to the new endpoint.
root_cause: After migrating the Zoom webhook endpoint to a new pub/sub-based architecture, the Zoom app started sending only a fraction of the expected events. The new endpoint appeared to work correctly for the events it received, but Zoom was simply not delivering all events. The team reverted to the old endpoint and planned a polling-based backup approach.
still_relevant: mitigated - Reverted to old endpoint, added Datadog alerts for low call analysis counts per org, planned polling-based backup to catch missing meetings.

what_broke: Google Calendar event syncing was failing with malformed API requests from Aug 4-7, 2023 (~3 days). The SYNC_GOOGLE_CALENDAR_UPCOMING_EVENTS and SYNC_GOOGLE_CALENDAR_MEETINGS schedulers were unable to fetch events, causing gaps in `salesforce_google_calendar_finished_event` and `salesforce_google_calendar_event` triggers. Affected workflows (mostly low priority) were not firing.
root_cause: A code regression where an optional method option was being added as a query parameter to Google Calendar API requests without checking if it was actually being used, resulting in malformed requests that Google rejected.
still_relevant: mitigated - Fix merged (PR #2611). The existing 7-day-ahead polling scheduler was expected to self-heal most of the gap, except for the weekend period.

what_broke: A scheduled job to delete orphan Salesforce flows unintentionally deleted valid Salesforce flows and outbound messages for multiple customer organizations.
root_cause: The logic to determine "orphaned" Salesforce flows was flawed -- when a FlowDefinition has multiple FlowIds (versions), the job incorrectly flagged active flows as orphaned and deleted them.
severity_detail: P1 (affected multiple customer orgs' Salesforce automation; required all-hands response and customer comms)
still_relevant: mitigated -- recreate job was built and dry-run gating added; however the underlying pattern of dangerous bulk-delete jobs without adequate safeguards could recur

what_broke: Momentum Kubernetes pods were repeatedly restarting due to failed liveness probe health checks, causing call summaries and notifications to fail or be delayed.
root_cause: Something was blocking the Node.js event loop for extended periods (likely heavy processing), causing the 1-second health check timeout to fail. Kubernetes then killed the pods.
severity_detail: P2 (call summaries failed/delayed for ~42 calls over a weekend; no permanent data loss after reprocessing)
still_relevant: mitigated -- liveness probe was relaxed and failed calls were reprocessed; underlying event loop blocking would need deeper fix

what_broke: Meeting/call imports from Gong were failing for multiple customer orgs. Calls were not getting their suggestedSummary populated, and some had duplicate key constraint violations on meeting_attendee records. Additionally, some calls appeared under wrong organizations.
root_cause: A meeting accessor update caused import failures. Duplicate sourceId entries across different organizations caused constraint violations. The system lacked tooling to detect and reprocess failed imports, making resolution painful.
severity_detail: P1 (multiple customer orgs affected; calls not being processed; manual per-call remediation required)
still_relevant: UNKNOWN (unclear from messages whether the underlying duplicate-org issue was fully resolved vs. manually patched per-call)

what_broke: Kubernetes pods were repeatedly crashing (CrashloopBackOff) in production due to unhandled `expired_trigger_id` errors from Slack API.
root_cause: When Slack actions (like approval modals) took more than 3 seconds to respond, Slack returned an `expired_trigger_id` error. This error was not caught, causing an uncaught exception that crashed the Node.js process/pod. Fix involved adding try-catch for `handleErrorAndShowModal` (PR #2837) and optimizing approval modal load times (PR #2841).
still_relevant: mitigated - fixed via error handling and performance optimization, channel archived Jan 2024

what_broke: Meeting sources were being incorrectly matched, causing existing meetings of the same type to clash. This prevented new Gong/Zoom calls from being imported and associated correctly, leading to missed call notifications for customers.
root_cause: A prior commit changed meeting source matching to use hostEmail + startTime instead of exact startTime matching, giving too much precedence to fuzzy matching. This caused Google Calendar events to incorrectly match and "claim" meeting slots, preventing real Gong/Zoom calls from being saved as meeting sources.
still_relevant: mitigated - fixed via improved source matching PR, added monitoring metric for same-type clashes, channel archived Jan 2024

what_broke: Significant mismatch between embeddings stored in the database and those actually present in Pinecone. About 40% of embeddings were missing from Pinecone, degrading AI features (Signals, Autopilot, AskMomentum).
root_cause: When indexing meeting transcripts, embeddings were sent to Pinecone in batches via OpenAI. The Pinecone upsert for subsequent batches would check if embeddings already existed, but due to Pinecone's eventual consistency, the check would find no existing embeddings and skip deduplication, resulting in only partial upserts. Fix was to add artificial lag between batch upserts.
still_relevant: mitigated - Fix was deployed (PR #2944, #2945, #2946) adding lag and a re-upsert scheduler with upsertedToPinecone tracking flag.

what_broke: Salesforce users across multiple customer organizations were being marked as unauthenticated due to an ongoing Salesforce incident (status.salesforce.com/incidents/11922). Google Cloud Monitoring alerts fired for the unauth events.
root_cause: Salesforce had a service incident that caused auth token validation to fail, leading Momentum to mark users as unauthenticated. The affected Salesforce instances did not include any Momentum customers directly, but the auth failures cascaded. Manual DB updates were required to revert users back to AUTHENTICATED status.
still_relevant: mitigated - Salesforce incident was resolved, but the system's sensitivity to transient auth failures from external services remains a concern.

what_broke: Database connectivity issues caused by RaiseAiSignalCreatedTrigger. Multiple flurries of DB timeout errors occurred, impacting overall system availability. This was the second time this specific issue occurred.
root_cause: The workflow engine used fire-and-forget pattern (FireAndForgetController.run) inside a forEach loop for new signal records, causing all signal triggers to run concurrently in the same process. This overwhelmed the database connection pool. Fix was PR #2959 to not fire-and-forget concurrent signal triggers.
still_relevant: mitigated - Fixed via PR #2959 (don't fire-and-forget concurrent signal triggers).

what_broke: Database CPU spiked to 100% and stayed elevated for over 24 hours, causing system-wide performance degradation. 81 concurrent DB processes were running a DELETE query on the "user" table.
root_cause: The PURGE_ORGANIZATION_DATA scheduled job ran a poorly optimized DELETE query (`DELETE FROM "user" WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM "user" WHERE "organizationId" = $1 LIMIT $2)`) without proper indexing. Multiple executions piled up when queries exceeded the scheduler interval. A subsequent index fix attempt made things worse.
severity_detail: P1 (DB at 100% CPU for 24+ hours; happened on weekend which limited customer impact)
still_relevant: mitigated -- job was paused, but the underlying query optimization and job locking still needed to be addressed

what_broke: Customer (Ramp) had all Salesforce outbound messages deleted, leaving Flows in an orphan state (Flows existed but their corresponding outbound messages did not), causing errors and inability to delete the orphaned Flows.
root_cause: Outbound messages were deleted on the Salesforce side (suspected customer action), but the corresponding Salesforce Flows were not cleaned up, creating orphaned references that the system could not handle or track.
still_relevant: mitigated - The system's resilience to SF-side deletions was identified as needing improvement, and orphan tracking was called out as a gap to fix.

what_broke: A Chowly customer's Salesforce "Case Upserted" events were generating massive volumes of processing, driving DB CPU to 100% and causing system-wide performance issues. The assist_action table had grown to over 4 million records without a needed index.
root_cause: Missing index on `assist_action.ref` column caused full table scans on a 4M+ row table. Chowly's high volume of case upsert events triggered many queries against this unindexed column, overwhelming the database. Chowly also hit Slack API rate limits.
severity_detail: P1 (DB CPU at 100%, system-wide impact, required emergency index creation and pod scaling)
still_relevant: mitigated -- index was added and migration action item was created; pattern of growing tables without proper indexes could recur

what_broke: Create notifications were erroring out for customers after a code deployment. A recent code change introduced a regression.
root_cause: A code deployment introduced a bug in the create notifications flow. The issue was resolved by reverting the problematic commit, and a postmortem was written.
still_relevant: mitigated - fixed via revert, postmortem documented, channel archived Jan 2024

what_broke: Gong call transcripts were not being imported for multiple customer orgs (notably 1Password). Calls that had both Zoom and Gong transcripts were silently failing to process.
root_cause: A bug in transcript import logic: when both a Zoom transcript and a Gong transcript were received for the same call, and the Zoom transcript was deemed to be skipped, the Gong transcript also failed to import. This caused call summaries and notifications to never reach Slack.
severity_detail: P2 (calls missing for ~1 week for affected customers; 35+ calls needed reimport for 1Password alone; no data loss since calls could be reimported)
still_relevant: mitigated -- bug was fixed and calls were reimported; no missed gong calls observed after fix

what_broke: Workflow notifications and calls were not being delivered to multiple customer organizations. Failed workflows affected at least 12 customer orgs including Zscaler (active POC), with up to 5 impacted workflows per org.
root_cause: A regression in the receiver-resolver send logic caused workflows to fail silently. The bug was initially not treated as an incident, and failed workflows were not rerun until it was escalated when Zscaler POC was affected. Required building a bulk rerun tool (PR #3123) to address at scale.
severity_detail: P1 (multiple customer orgs affected; impacted active POC with Zscaler; required customer communication and bulk rerun across 12+ orgs)
still_relevant: mitigated -- bug was fixed, bulk rerun tool built, all affected workflows rerun across all orgs

what_broke: Workflow conditions on call analysis were being inverted -- workflows that should only trigger for specific hosts were triggering for ALL calls except those hosts. This caused calls to be analyzed and notifications sent when they should not have been.
root_cause: The frontend condition builder was changed to remove the `equal to` operator for emails, replacing it with `includes one of`. When users who had existing `equal to` conditions edited their workflow (even without touching the condition), the condition was re-rendered incorrectly, turning `(email === 'x')` into `(\!['x'].includes(email))`, which inverts the logic. Affected DemandBase and Weights & Biases.
severity_detail: P2 (two customer orgs affected; incorrect call analysis sent but no data loss; condition builder bug could have spread to more customers)
still_relevant: mitigated -- bug was fixed with a PR, DB query confirmed no other active workflows had the broken condition pattern

what_broke: Database was under extreme load causing timeouts across the web app, workflow processing delays, and Google Calendar event processing backlog (5.5k+ messages backed up in PubSub). Hasura remote-schema errors were also occurring. The Node.js event loop was being blocked.
root_cause: High database load from multiple compounding factors: slow GCal event processing creating a backlog, DB queries timing out, event loop blocking (GC pauses over 1s), and PubSub message backlogs across SFDC and GCal. Multiple calls failed to fire `meeting_transcript_complete` triggers (34 total from Zoom, Gong, Wingman). Also compounded by Pinecone issues.
severity_detail: P0 (system-wide degradation; web app timing out; workflow processing delayed; multiple days to fully resolve)
still_relevant: mitigated -- resolved after multi-day effort including reducing parallel event processing, clearing PubSub backlog, and rerunning 34 failed calls; post-mortem planned for architectural improvements

what_broke: Slack notifications stopped working properly -- updating Salesforce objects from Slack modals failed (sometimes running multiple times on retry), and Slack modals were not showing up. Users could not interact with deal rooms or update opportunities via Slack.
root_cause: A code change introduced a bug where a database select query was missing required columns. Specifically, when fetching an `AssistAction`, the `select` clause did not include all necessary fields (like `customAuthUser.id`), causing downstream failures when trying to process Slack interactions and modal submissions.
severity_detail: P0 (core Slack interaction functionality broken; customer-reported; marked as P0 in channel topic)
still_relevant: mitigated -- fix deployed, incident report created; recommendation to use `Pick` types for all select queries to prevent similar issues

what_broke: Salesforce OAuth tokens became invalid for multiple customers, causing Salesforce integration failures. Token refresh was returning "invalid_grant" / "inactive organization" errors, blocking the Salesforce event processing queue for all customers.
root_cause: During migration from OneGraph to Momentum's own Salesforce OAuth client, some users were missed for re-authentication. Additionally, Salesforce returned a transient "inactive organization" error that incorrectly invalidated tokens.
still_relevant: mitigated - OneGraph was deprecated and auth verify handler was proposed for Salesforce (already existed for Zoom)

what_broke: System-wide latency and timeouts in the platform, causing workflow failures. 57 out of 6035 workflow runs failed. A pod died due to stack overflow from Datadog stack trace generation.
root_cause: Garbage collection pressure caused by the workflow engine's recursive execution pattern, combined with large stack traces being generated for Datadog. A large Error.stackTraceLimit (40) and deep workflow recursion created excessive memory pressure.
still_relevant: UNKNOWN - unclear if GC/recursion issues were structurally addressed

what_broke: Slack Direct Message action in workflows was failing with "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')". 754 Slack DM actions failed across ~27 customer organizations.
root_cause: A code regression introduced via a PR that was merged without sufficient review. The code accessed a property on a potentially undefined value. Lack of unit tests in this critical path allowed the bug to ship.
still_relevant: mitigated - fix was deployed, failed actions were rerun. Action items included adding unit tests and better alerting for elevated workflow error rates.

what_broke: Salesforce flow events, Google Meet/Calendar events, and Zoom events were failing to be processed. GCP Pub/Sub publish calls were timing out with "Total timeout of API google.pubsub.v1.Publisher exceeded 60000 milliseconds". Schedulers also failed. Deployments were stuck with pods in pending state.
root_cause: Google Cloud Platform outage in us-west1 region affecting multiple products including Load Balancers, Pub/Sub, and GKE. GCP identified a common infrastructure component as root cause.
still_relevant: yes - team noted need for multi-zone architecture for event receivers and Pub/Sub to mitigate single-region GCP failures

what_broke: Call summaries were failing to post across all customers with error "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'meeting')". 60 failed workflows across 16 orgs.
root_cause: A code change moved `settings` handling in the template engine to work like the Slack User dictionary (not stored in context anymore). Direct references to `context.settings` broke, causing all summary workflows to fail.
still_relevant: mitigated - fix deployed, failed workflows rerun

what_broke: Summary DM notifications were being incorrectly marked as duplicates, preventing delivery to users. Affected ~10 customer organizations with ~212 messages needing rerun.
root_cause: A regression merged on 2024-03-12 in the `hasDuplicateMessage` utility function did not query for `slackUserId` when checking for duplicate Direct Messages. Because summaries reuse `call.suggestedSummary`, messages with the same hash but different recipients were incorrectly flagged as duplicates.
still_relevant: mitigated - fix deployed, DM notifications added to testing checklist

what_broke: Internal calls at ZScaler (and potentially other orgs) were being recorded by Momentum when they should not have been. Zoom Personal Meeting Rooms (PMIs) were left with recording enabled after an external meeting ended, causing subsequent internal meetings using the same PMI to be recorded. Additionally, Google conference room domains were incorrectly classified as external attendees, further mislabeling calls.
root_cause: Two issues: (1) Zoom recording was not being disabled on PMIs after the target meeting ended, so any subsequent meeting in that room was also recorded. (2) Google conference room domains were counted as external attendees, causing internal-only meetings to be classified as external. 5 ZScaler users were affected.
still_relevant: mitigated - fix deployed to disable recording on meeting.ended event, and to exclude conference room domains from external attendee classification

what_broke: Gladia (transcription vendor) usage spiked to 55K+ hours, raising alarm about unexpected billing. Investigation showed transcription requests with extremely long audio_duration values (94K+ seconds for a single request).
root_cause: This was a vendor-side billing bug at Gladia/Recall. The high usage numbers were incorrect on Gladia's reporting. After Gladia fixed their bug, usage dropped from 55K+ to 325, which was normal. No actual issue on Momentum's side.
still_relevant: no - was a Gladia billing bug, not a real incident

what_broke: Call summary notifications were failing for multiple orgs (primarily ZScaler) because the notification receiver field was empty. Users were not receiving their call summaries. 124+ actions went into halted state with "slack receiver is empty" errors.
root_cause: Stale Redis cache keys caused Salesforce user lookups to return outdated/incorrect data. Users with multiple Salesforce userIDs (some inactive) were being resolved incorrectly. A fix was deployed to overwrite inactive Salesforce userIDs in the cache, but edge cases persisted for users duplicated for other reasons.
still_relevant: mitigated - fix deployed to overwrite inactive SF userIDs, but some edge cases with duplicate users remained

what_broke: Zoom meeting processing was failing with `QueryFailedError: value "98017503074" is out of range for type integer`. Zoom meetings with IDs exceeding the integer range could not be stored or processed. Additionally, the `disableZoomRecording` function was using the Zoom meetingId in place of organizationId to look up organizations, causing further failures.
root_cause: The database column type for Zoom meeting IDs was `integer` (max ~2.1 billion), but Zoom had started issuing meeting IDs that exceeded this range (e.g., 98017503074). The column needed to be changed to `bigint`. A secondary bug was found where meetingId was incorrectly passed as organizationId in the disableZoomRecording function.
still_relevant: mitigated - column type was migrated to bigint and the parameter bug was fixed

what_broke: The momentum-scheduler service could not refresh OAuth tokens for Google Calendar and other integrations, causing meetings to not be synced/recorded for affected users over a Thursday-Monday period. 19 meetings were missed across ~10 affected users.
root_cause: A bug in the scheduler's token refresh logic threw "No callback registered for service" errors, preventing OAuth tokens from being refreshed. The scheduler used a different code path than momentum-api, so only background calendar sync was affected.
severity_detail: P1 (customer-facing missed call recordings for multiple orgs including Zscaler)
still_relevant: mitigated - bug was fixed and the scheduler code was updated; also mentioned effort to scale and remove bottlenecks from main app

what_broke: Salesforce flow event receiver was rejecting incoming Salesforce outbound messages with HTTP 413 (Content Too Large) errors. Affected 5+ customer organizations (Ramp, mParticle, Jasper, Owner, Culture Amp). Workflows using large field payloads were silently failing.
root_cause: Nginx proxy in front of the salesforce-flow-event-receiver had a client_body_buffer_size of 100K, but some Salesforce outbound message payloads were up to ~1.9MB (containing many fields). Customers with workflows referencing many fields hit this limit.
severity_detail: P1 (multiple customers' Salesforce workflows silently failing)
still_relevant: mitigated - discussion about increasing the limit and also moving workflows to use outboundMessageFieldNames to reduce payload sizes

what_broke: Workflows failed with error "receivedAttachments is not iterable" when processing Salesforce events. Multiple organizations affected with failed assist actions.
root_cause: A code change (PR #3754) assumed receivedAttachments and threadedBlocks parameters would always be arrays, but some code paths passed null as the parameter value, causing iteration to fail.
severity_detail: P1 (workflows failing across multiple orgs)
still_relevant: mitigated - fix was deployed and failed workflows were manually re-run

what_broke: Replaying salesforce.flow triggers for a customer (Radar, org 127) caused an avalanche of unintended workflow executions - ~500 salesforce triggers were replayed and dealrooms were incorrectly created for channels that shouldn't have received them (~27 erroneous dealrooms created before the pod was killed).
root_cause: The trigger replay endpoint didn't have access to the specific flow/outbound message context, so any workflow with trigger type salesforce.flow was executed for all replayed triggers, not just the intended ones. Additionally, a regression may have been introduced when migrating away from opportunity_updated trigger type.
severity_detail: P1 (customer-facing incorrect dealroom creation, required cleanup)
still_relevant: mitigated - pod was killed, erroneous dealrooms were identified for cleanup; replay endpoint still lacks proper trigger-to-workflow filtering

what_broke: A regression caused errors in the `elevateInternalAttendeeToHost` function during call processing. GONG, WINGMAN, and RECALL call syncs were affected, and Google Calendar event sync was also impacted across multiple organizations.
root_cause: A code regression that was deployed to production caused errors when processing users that aren't soft-deleted. The `elevateInternalAttendeeToHost` function was throwing errors during call processing. The specific regression likely related to user status checking logic.
severity_detail: P1 (call processing failures across multiple orgs, Google Calendar sync affected)
still_relevant: mitigated - fix was deployed, GONG/WINGMAN calls were re-imported, and Google Calendar sync was re-run for all orgs

what_broke: Analyze transcript was failing for orgs with custom summaries. The CallSuggestSummary action was throwing TemplateReferenceError because Salesforce objects (Opportunity, account, salesforceHostUser) were not defined in the evaluation context. 28+ actions failed across orgs 202, 216, and 162 (Pigment, Amplemarket, Spare).
root_cause: Custom summary conditions referenced Salesforce objects (e.g., Opportunity, account) that were not included in the call summary condition context. The trigger context for meeting_transcript_complete did not include Salesforce objects, and a fix (PR #4044) only addressed one trigger path but not the graphQL resolver trigger. A second fix (PR #4046) was needed to properly add context to the action evaluation.
severity_detail: P1 (custom summaries failing for multiple customer orgs)
still_relevant: mitigated - fix deployed (PR #4046), failed actions retried and completed successfully

what_broke: Pinecone Serverless was not indexing vectors within the 60-second timeout, causing PineconeServerlessClient.upsertVectors failures. 189 actions initially failed with "Wait for searchable by id timeout" (30%) and "Wait for searchable by metadata timeout" (70%).
root_cause: Pinecone Serverless had degraded performance causing vectors to not become searchable within the expected 60-second window. Pinecone also had a concurrent outage where "Some queries are incorrectly returning no results." The system only had 3 auto-retries which was insufficient for transient Pinecone issues.
severity_detail: P1 (widespread indexing failures across all orgs using Pinecone)
still_relevant: mitigated - switched back to legacy Pinecone indexing, bumped auto-retries to 6, shared code with Pinecone for investigation. Linear ticket ENG-7042 created.

what_broke: Call summaries were not getting posted for already processed meetings. The call_analyzed trigger was failing with "Cannot find object metadata for organization 1 Salesforce object with oneGraphifiedName Opportunity." Multiple issues were conflated: a contains function bug (affecting Aampe and Amplemarket), a oneGraph migration issue (affecting Momentum org 1), and Zscaler summary failures.
root_cause: AI Summary workflows were migrated off oneGraphified syntax, but the code that resolved Salesforce object names (salesforceObjectNameFromOneGraphified) still expected the old format. When a root object name was non-oneGraphified, it used the wrong controller. Fix was PR #4064: "use non-oneGraphified controller when root object name is non-oneGraphified."
severity_detail: P2 (initially scoped as P3 since only Momentum org was confirmed affected, but Zscaler was also impacted)
still_relevant: mitigated - fix deployed, call_analyzed triggers replayed for affected orgs

what_broke: Pinecone Serverless experienced an outage causing query/fetch operations to fail in AWS us-west-2 with PineconeUnmappedHttpError (503 errors). 89+ failures with PineconeUnmappedHttpError, plus additional failures from indexing timeouts. This compounded with the previous Pinecone Serverless timeout issues from July 26.
root_cause: Pinecone Serverless had two separate outages: (1) "Query/Fetch operations failing in AWS us-west-2 serverless" and (2) "Elevated latencies and gateway errors (504) for control plane APIs." Additionally, the non-serverless Pinecone cluster was 90%+ full with 8x pods (the max), creating pressure to use Serverless despite its reliability issues.
severity_detail: P1 (widespread call processing failures, indexing blocked across all orgs)
still_relevant: mitigated - stopped indexing to Pinecone Serverless, switched search back to non-serverless via feature flag. However, non-serverless cluster at capacity (90%+ full, max 8x pods) remains a structural risk.

what_broke: Digest/brief LLM requests were generated with missing transcript data due to unresolved template variables, resulting in low-quality briefs sent to customers across 54 digest instances (21 orgs).
root_cause: A bug in template variable resolution caused transcript data to not be injected into LLM prompts for digest generation, leaving template placeholders in the requests.
still_relevant: mitigated — fix deployed and unit tests added; bad instances were soft-deleted and re-run

what_broke: Email embedding indexing was deleting existing meeting transcript embeddings from Pinecone for the same Salesforce account/opportunity, degrading LLM request context quality (Ask Momentum, briefs) for 6 customer orgs.
root_cause: When upserting email-body embeddings to Pinecone, the system deleted all existing embeddings for the same salesforce entity without filtering by sourceType, thus removing meeting-transcript embeddings.
still_relevant: mitigated — code fix deployed, ~23,621 embedding sets re-upserted to Pinecone, and indexes added to embedding_set table

what_broke: The momentum-scheduler pod entered a CrashLoopBackOff state. A Zoom recording download was failing with ECONNRESET, and the unhandled error crashed the pod. Additionally, a stalled assist in `created` status kept being retried, creating assist_actions with unresolved template variables (`{{meetingId}}`).
root_cause: A code change in Zoom recording download did not gracefully handle ECONNRESET errors from the Zoom API, causing the download pipeline to crash the pod. The stalled assist retry logic then continuously restarted the problematic workflow.
still_relevant: mitigated — the code change was reverted, the stalled assist was manually set to failed, and a postmortem was written

what_broke: Approximately 136 meetings over the past month failed to have their summaries posted to Slack. The meeting import process skipped triggering the "call analyzed" event because it read from the database read replica before the data was replicated.
root_cause: Read replica lag caused the `triggerMeetingTranscriptComplete` logic to read stale data, finding no external attendees (because the attendee records hadn't replicated yet), and therefore skipping the summary posting workflow.
still_relevant: mitigated — fix deployed to not use read replica for this business logic; 105/135 affected calls successfully reimported

what_broke: Database CPU spiked to unusually high levels, causing performance degradation across the platform. The workflow engine was overwhelmed with tens of thousands of triggers from a single customer (Ramp).
root_cause: Enabling email ingestion for Ramp (org 139) flooded the system with ~22,000 salesforce.flow triggers and ~19,500 email_saved triggers in 3 hours, overwhelming the workflow engine and database.
still_relevant: mitigated — email workflows disabled for Ramp; feature flag added to control email ingestion rollout; additional flow condition filtering added

what_broke: A code change caused meeting import and Google Calendar event processing to fail with a null reference error ("Cannot read properties of undefined ... forEach") when saving structured transcripts. This affected meeting transcript completion triggers, Zoom recording enablement, and Salesloft transcription starts for ~1.5 hours.
root_cause: A missing null check in `createOrUpdateMeetingFromSourceDetails` for the `meeting.transcripts` relation caused an exception when some meetings had no transcripts array. The error occurred after saving the meeting but before triggering downstream processing.
still_relevant: mitigated — fix-forward deployed adding null check for transcripts array; impact assessed as minimal (11 calls without summaries, no missed recordings, no missed Salesloft transcriptions)

what_broke: OpenAI API experienced a major outage, causing all LLM-dependent workflows (meeting summaries, signals, ask momentum, etc.) to fail with 500/502 errors. Some workflows exhausted their 6 auto-retries.
root_cause: OpenAI had a widespread API and ChatGPT outage lasting several hours. Momentum's workflows that depend on OpenAI failed and queued up.
still_relevant: mitigated — OpenAI recovered; failed actions with exhausted retries were manually reset and rerun; action item noted to invest in cross-vendor AI execution (Gemini/Anthropic) for resilience

what_broke: Database CPU spiked to 100%, causing widespread performance degradation. Long-running queries (4-14 min each) on the `ai_generated_text` table caused table-locking and cascading failures.
root_cause: JustWorks had a bulk update of 10,000+ Salesforce Opportunities in under 40 minutes, which triggered retropilot workflows. The AI Signals feature used the `ai_generated_text` table with stringified IDs and no proper indexes, causing full table scans. Combined with the burst of ~24K trigger payloads from JustWorks, this overwhelmed the database.
still_relevant: mitigated — temporary index added to ai_generated_text; Signals v2 prioritized with better design; need to stop stringifying IDs and add proper indexing

what_broke: The SYNC_RECALL_BOTS scheduler job was failing with a TypeError ("Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceType')"), preventing Recall bots from being dispatched to record meetings. This went undetected for ~12 days (Jan 24 to Feb 5), causing potentially 2,065 missed recordings for Zscaler alone, plus ~300 across other orgs.
root_cause: A code regression introduced a null reference error in the sync recall bots handler when a meeting source had undefined properties. The error caused the entire scheduler job to fail silently for affected organizations.
still_relevant: mitigated — fix deployed; impact measured (910 potentially unrecorded Zscaler meetings, ~300 others); log alert added for "SyncRecallBots.handler: Error adding bot to meeting"; postmortem conducted

what_broke: Recall.ai (external meeting bot vendor) experienced an API outage in us-east region, causing meeting bots to fail to join calls and returning 500 errors when Momentum tried to sync transcripts.
root_cause: Recall.ai had heavy database lock contention at the top of each hour causing a death spiral; all database requests failed for ~4 hours (16:58-21:00 UTC).
still_relevant: yes -- Recall.ai remains an external dependency and similar outages could recur. No retry mechanism for "Invite Momentum" bot additions.

what_broke: Zoom DNS misconfiguration caused 502 Bad Gateway errors on Zoom API calls, preventing Momentum from enabling meeting recording (PATCH requests) and fetching transcripts (GET requests) for ~2 hours. 37 meetings went into FAILED_TO_PRE_MEETING_ENABLE state across 11 orgs.
root_cause: Zoom had a DNS misconfiguration on the zoom.us domain that caused multiple services to be unreachable. Momentum's PATCH calls to enable recording and GET calls for transcripts all returned 502 errors.
still_relevant: yes -- Zoom remains an external dependency; no automatic recovery for FAILED_TO_PRE_MEETING_ENABLE meetings.

what_broke: A second Zoom outage caused Zoom OAuth refresh tokens to become invalid for some users, resulting in AUTHENTICATION_REQUIRED status and FAILED_TO_PRE_MEETING_ENABLE for 6 meetings across Zscaler and Runwise.
root_cause: Zoom connectivity outage likely invalidated/tampered with single-use refresh tokens. When Momentum attempted to refresh auth tokens, Zoom returned 400 "Invalid Grant" errors, forcing users into AUTHENTICATION_REQUIRED state requiring manual re-auth.
still_relevant: yes -- Zoom refresh token fragility remains; no automated recovery when tokens are invalidated by upstream outages.

what_broke: Data insight extractions started outputting raw JSON reasoning text as field values instead of properly parsed values. Affected ~10,993 data_insight records across 57+ organizations.
root_cause: A PR (#5336) introduced truncation of LLM output before JSON parsing, causing the JSON to be malformed/truncated. The LLM's reasoning text was being stored as the extracted value instead of the actual parsed value.
still_relevant: mitigated -- PR was reverted, fix deployed on April 29 at 10:40 AM PDT. Impact assessment and remediation of existing flawed extractions was needed.

what_broke: Veriff meetings were missing recording consent URLs because calendar event imports failed to parse conference details (crossSourceAttributes). At least 14 meetings for Veriff alone were affected since April 1.
root_cause: Read replica eventual consistency caused a race condition: when a calendar event was imported and updated quickly with conference details, the second import read from the replica before the first write propagated, resulting in crossSourceAttributes being wiped. The scheduler sync would never reprocess the same etag.
still_relevant: mitigated -- Fix deployed to read from primary DB for recording consent check (PR #5497). Underlying race condition in calendar event processing may still need broader fix.

what_broke: Meeting processing (Google Calendar event imports, Recall transcript syncs, Zoom event processing) failed with "QueryFailedError: canceling statement due to conflict with recovery" errors. 5 Google Calendar events and 5 Recall meetings affected, plus Zoom events for orgs 49 and 284.
root_cause: A code change moved a query to use EntityController.getOne which defaults to the read replica. The read replica has a 30-second hard cutoff for long-running queries, causing conflicts with recovery and statement cancellation.
still_relevant: mitigated -- Helm rollback performed, then rolled forward with fix (PR #5546 adding index on user.fullName). Manual SQL needed to recover affected meetings.

what_broke: A bug in the recording architecture update caused the scheduler to not record calls via Zoom and Recall. Hundreds of meetings across 30+ organizations were not recorded over several days (May 26-30).
root_cause: A bug in the algorithm for recording architecture caused meetings to remain in TO_BE_ENABLED/invalid recallAutoRecordState, preventing Zoom and Recall from recording calls. The scheduler failed to transition meetings to the proper recording state.
still_relevant: mitigated -- Fix applied, future meetings not affected. Recordings lost during the incident window are unrecoverable. Hard to monitor due to variable call trends.

what_broke: Retropilot (AI-powered Salesforce field extraction) produced degraded outputs for over a month (May 13 - June 19, 2025), affecting 29 organizations. Some retropilot tasks were skipped or generated lower-quality outputs.
root_cause: A regression in PR #5527 caused the embedding search endpoint to use the creation date of the backfill job instead of the backfill task when filtering embeddings, resulting in relevant context being excluded from retropilot processing.
still_relevant: mitigated -- Regression resolved for outputs going forward. Impacted organizations offered reruns of skipped tasks, though results may be stale.

what_broke: Meeting recording consent source updates started returning 400 errors, triggered by a Google Cloud Monitoring alert on the scheduler pod.
root_cause: UNKNOWN from the 30 messages available, but a fix was deployed on July 7, 2025 and the issue was resolved with no further alerts. A postmortem was written (Notion link shared).
still_relevant: mitigated -- Fix deployed, postmortem completed with corrective and preventive measures identified.

what_broke: Non-lower-case emails in the user table caused license lookup failures. When comparing emails from call transcripts (e.g., Gong) to find meeting hosts, the case mismatch caused users not to be identified, breaking meeting processing for affected organizations (92, 319, 323, 329).
root_cause: Some user emails were stored with mixed case (camelCase) in the database, while input data from call providers used lowercase. The system lacked a constraint to enforce lowercase email storage, and case-sensitive comparisons in license lookup functions (fromHostEmailAddress, someEmailAddressesLicensed) failed to match.
still_relevant: mitigated -- Migration applied to fix affected records, and a CHECK constraint added on user.email to enforce lowercase going forward (PR #5959). Postmortem completed.

what_broke: Customer-managed Salesforce flow processing broke after a deployment that moved Salesforce-based flows to Temporal. Zscaler meetings invited from Salesforce were not processed correctly (user-provided flows/sources not being created).
root_cause: Deployment of Salesforce flow migration to Temporal did not account for user-defined (customer-managed) Salesforce flows. The salesforceFlow was not being fetched from the wizard as a fallback, causing a regression.
still_relevant: mitigated - fix deployed (PR #5968), integration test added (PR #5989), postmortem completed. Affected ~208 meetings for Zscaler.

what_broke: Customers including Ramp and Datarails reported workflows and extractions not firing due to lack of Opportunity mappings in the system.
root_cause: UNKNOWN (channel shows investigation was started but root cause details not discussed in channel messages; Gong mapping/remapping mentioned as a todo)
still_relevant: UNKNOWN - channel still open as of latest messages, follow-up question asked about whether issue persists and whether missing linking reason was resolved.

what_broke: Autopilot Classic (data insights) failed to find the relevant Salesforce object, causing the majority of Autopilot Classic jobs to be skipped for ~20 hours across 75+ customer orgs.
root_cause: Changes to support 'Salesforce paths' in data insights altered how Autopilot Classic fetches the Salesforce object to write to. Any Autopilot Classic job without a 'Salesforce path' (the majority) was skipped.
still_relevant: mitigated - fix deployed, reruns completed for 68+ customer orgs, postmortem written. Channel archived.

what_broke: Internal workflows were missing their corresponding Salesforce flows. The system failed to create Salesforce flows for internal assist_definitions, affecting multiple customer organizations.
root_cause: When creating Salesforce flows for internal workflows, if the org had no users configured to make Salesforce requests on its behalf, the flow creation failed silently without retry. This was a pre-existing bug (not a regression) in the Internals.ts code path.
still_relevant: mitigated - fix applied by deleting affected assist_definitions and letting the scheduler recreate them. Feature flag (OrganizationFeatureSet) added to make internal workflows optional. Retry logic being added for Salesforce flow creation errors.

what_broke: The `insertOne` database method was not returning the ID of the inserted record, affecting data insight extraction (Autopilot), Slack task creation, and integration test setup.
root_cause: JavaScript operator precedence bug: `return await this.insert(entity, organizationId, [row])[0]` was indexing the Promise rather than the resolved array. Fix was to add parentheses: `return (await this.insert(entity, organizationId, [row]))[0]`.
still_relevant: mitigated - bug fix deployed. Permanent resolution involves resuming the migration effort of questions to promptIds and handling it behind the main endpoint.

what_broke: Recall (external meeting bot provider) was unable to send bots to meetings, causing meetings to not be recorded. Approximately 105 meetings across 13 customer orgs were affected.
root_cause: Recall exceeded an AWS rate limit, preventing bot launches. Bots failed with 507 errors when attempting to join meetings.
still_relevant: mitigated - issue resolved upstream by Recall/provider. Failed bots in RECALL_FAILED_TO_ADD_BOT state were retried by scheduler. Postmortem requested.

what_broke: Elevated false-positive source matching was occurring based on meeting title using Levenshtein distance (fuzzy matching). Google Calendar sources were being incorrectly matched to meetings, particularly affecting Zscaler.
root_cause: A code change to source matching conditions introduced fuzzy title matching (Levenshtein distance) for calendar sources (Google Calendar/Outlook), which was only appropriate for Zoom sources. This caused incorrect matches between unrelated meetings with similar titles.
still_relevant: mitigated - the change was reverted (PR #6121), and a targeted fix was planned to only apply fuzzy matching for Zoom vs Outlook/GCal. Postmortem completed.

what_broke: Video asset import was skipped for meetings, causing recordings to not be associated with processed meetings. More than 149 meetings across 30+ customer orgs were affected.
root_cause: A code change caused video asset import to be skipped during meeting processing. The exact change was later redeployed with a fix. Additionally, a missing `meeting.recordingStatus` field meant there was no scheduler to catch expected-but-unimported recordings.
still_relevant: mitigated - fix deployed, all affected meetings were re-imported. Planning to add `meeting.recordingStatus` for scheduler-based detection.

what_broke: LLM-based attendee consolidation was removing legitimate attendees from meetings. The LLM was "scrubbing" (modifying) attendee names, and when the modified names were compared to the original attendees via exact match, they failed to match and were discarded. This caused meetings to have no external attendees and not be processed. About 87 meetings affected, primarily Zscaler.
root_cause: The LLM attendee consolidation task modified attendee names/displayNames, and the code in `attendee.ts#L250` compared the LLM-modified names against original attendees using exact matching on name/displayName/email. Modified names failed to match, causing legitimate attendees to be dropped.
still_relevant: mitigated - LLM consolidation feature flag disabled, affected meetings rerun. Fix involves avoiding exact match on LLM-scrubbed name/displayName. Feature was re-enabled with fix for all orgs.

what_broke: Gladia (transcription provider used by Recall) had an outage that prevented call transcription, resulting in meetings with NO_TRANSCRIPT status. 30 meetings across multiple orgs (primarily Zscaler) were affected with missing transcripts.
root_cause: External Gladia outage caused transcription failures. Additionally, when Recall re-processed bots, a database constraint error (`IDX_recall_bot_history_botId`) prevented re-syncing because the bots were already in the `recall_bot_history` table.
still_relevant: mitigated - Recall reran transcription for affected bots, transcript import was triggered for affected meetings. Postmortem written. Action item: add error handling when Gladia fails to transcribe during bot sync.

what_broke: Coaching aggregate meeting scores were being saved as zero for all meetings processed after 2025-08-21. 1597 CoachingAggregateInstance records across 7 orgs were affected.
root_cause: A regression introduced by a PR merged on 2025-08-21 caused the aggregate score calculation for meetings to stop updating, setting all scores to 0.
severity_detail: P1 (customer-facing data corruption across multiple orgs, reported by customer Tebra)
still_relevant: mitigated -- fix deployed, support API ran to recalculate scores, Salesforce records updated

what_broke: Gladia (external transcription provider) had an API outage from Sep 2 8:41 PM to Sep 3 1:15 AM, resulting in calls being processed without transcripts. 14 bots affected.
root_cause: Gladia external service outage caused their API to be unavailable, leading to missing transcripts for calls that had speaker timelines.
severity_detail: P2 (calls missing transcripts during outage window, detected early due to prior incident learnings)
still_relevant: mitigated -- monitor created for NO_TRANSCRIPT_WITH_SPEAKER_TIMELINE state spikes; re-ran transcription jobs for affected bots

what_broke: 814 Gong calls across 6 orgs were not processed because Zoom sources were imported instead, blocking the Gong source import for those meetings.
root_cause: A Zoom license check was removed during attendee licensing implementation, allowing Zoom sources to be imported for orgs that should only have had Gong sources imported. The imported Zoom sources (without matching calendar events) created meetings that were never processed, and prevented Gong sources from being imported.
severity_detail: P1 (814 calls missed processing across 6 orgs, customer 1Password escalated)
still_relevant: mitigated -- calls reimported, Linear ticket INT-79 created to update licensing logic during Zoom source import

what_broke: 66 customer calls (60 from Zscaler alone) that should have been recorded were missed because Recall's bot creation API was returning 400 errors.
root_cause: Recall (external vendor) introduced a regression in their bot schema (v1.10 APIs) during an internal migration to standardize bot schema, causing deserialization failures and bot creation failures for v1.10 clients. The outage lasted from 14:14 UTC to 23:57 UTC on Sep 9.
severity_detail: P1 (66 missed call recordings across 4 orgs including key customer Zscaler)
still_relevant: mitigated -- Recall deployed fix, failure rates back to sub-1%; action items include enabling payload logging for outbound Recall API requests and reviewing alert thresholds

what_broke: Salesforce authentication was failing for customers. Users could not authorize the Momentum Salesforce integration; the `momentum-production-v2` Connected App was not visible or accessible for some orgs. Multiple customers affected (Zscaler, League, Clipboard, Conexiom, Cin7, Spark Hire, Trolley).
root_cause: UNKNOWN from messages alone -- appears related to Salesforce Connected App setup/configuration issues. Customers had difficulty finding and authorizing the `momentum-production-v2` app. Some orgs had restrictive access controls requiring explicit user allow-listing. Investigation ongoing into migrating to Salesforce External Client App to simplify setup.
severity_detail: P1 (multiple customers affected, onboarding blocked for some, ongoing for weeks)
still_relevant: yes -- investigating migration to Salesforce External Client App to eliminate extra setup steps; multiple customers still affected at time of channel activity

what_broke: Workflow conditions contained "onegraphified" (internal/legacy) field names instead of proper Salesforce field names, causing workflows to fail execution. Affected workflows across many orgs including AI Call Summary, AI Playground, and advanced workflows.
root_cause: A migration or code change left legacy "onegraphified" field names in workflow conditions (e.g., referencing Salesforce objects like Opportunity). These internal field names were not valid at runtime, causing workflow execution failures with errors like "salesforceHostUser is not defined".
severity_detail: P2 (workflow execution failures across many orgs, but required manual fix per org/workflow)
still_relevant: mitigated -- manual fixes applied across orgs 1-162, fix deployed for the underlying code; suggestion made to add alerts if workflows get misconfigured again

what_broke: Signal v2 Slack notifications were failing when Momentum was not added to the target Slack channel, causing the entire `meetingProcessTranscript` workflow to fail. Affected orgs 1, 139, 230, and 284 with many workflow failures over 30 days.
root_cause: A known bug was not resolved before the Signals v2 feature was rolled out to customers. When a Signal v2 notification tried to post to a Slack channel where Momentum was not a member, it threw a `not_in_channel` error that was not handled gracefully, causing the parent workflow to fail entirely.
severity_detail: P2 (workflow failures across 4 orgs, signals not firing, concern about old signals firing during re-runs)
still_relevant: mitigated -- fix deployed (PR #6428), failed workflows re-run for affected orgs

what_broke: Slack interactions (listing opportunities in dropdowns, pushing data from Slack to Salesforce) were failing for multiple customers including Ramp, Demandbase, Snorkel, and Spekit. Users could not see opportunities in Slack dropdown lists or complete Salesforce actions from Slack.
root_cause: The `initializeContext` Slack middleware was selecting soft-deleted users instead of active users when resolving Slack user IDs, causing authorization/context failures. A fix was deployed to stop fetching soft-deleted users (PR #6593). Additionally, there may have been issues related to Slack user+team ID extraction changes deployed around the same time.
severity_detail: P1 (multiple customers blocked from core Slack functionality for over a week, key accounts Ramp and Demandbase affected)
still_relevant: mitigated -- fix deployed (PR #6593 "Stop fetching soft-deleted users for slack initialize context"), confirmed working for Ramp; Spekit still reporting issues pushing to Salesforce

what_broke: Calls on Google Meet were sporadically not being recorded by the Momentum bot. The scheduler responsible for adding recording bots to meetings was timing out and missing eligible calls. Multiple orgs affected with hundreds of missed recordings (141 on Sep 19, more on Sep 22).
root_cause: The call recording scheduler was hitting time limits, causing it to miss adding bots to eligible meetings. The scheduler was processing too many meetings in a single run. Fixes included optimizing the scheduler query, adding monitoring alerts for scheduler timeouts, and proposals for an hourly catchup scheduler and PENDING_BOT_ADDITION state.
severity_detail: P0 (hundreds of calls not recorded across multiple orgs, customer comms required for Owner and Veriff, ongoing for multiple days)
still_relevant: mitigated -- scheduler optimizations deployed, monitoring alerts added; still being tracked on Sep 22+ with ongoing verification

what_broke: Momentum's "Ask" feature and other Slack interactions were failing in shared Slack channels. Slack events from shared channels were being rejected or misrouted, causing features like reaction-based workflows and Ask to stop working in shared channels used by CS teams with customers.
root_cause: A recent security update to how Slack events extract User + Team IDs introduced a regression. The new algorithm (based on Slack's own recommendations) was overly restrictive for shared channels, rejecting valid events. Additionally, there was a cross-org security concern where Slack User/Team matching in shared channels could potentially map events to the wrong organization. Multiple iterative fixes were required (PR #6509, #6554, #6570).
severity_detail: P1 (shared channel functionality broken, actively used by CS team with customers, security concern identified)
still_relevant: mitigated -- multiple fixes deployed over Sep 22-29 to handle shared channel edge cases securely; officially supporting shared channels with client org + active Momentum employees only

what_broke: Meeting processing and summary posting were delayed for ~5 hours. Meetings with Smart Clips running and Signal v2 triggers were affected.
root_cause: Hit the GCP Transcoding API quota limit (batch concurrent job count per region was capped at 10 in us-west1), causing video processing to stall.
still_relevant: `mitigated` - Quota increased to 100, monitoring added, and decoupling Smart Clips from general meeting processing was planned.

what_broke: Zscaler (customer) SFDC integration disconnected, causing meeting bot failures. Two separate incidents: (1) Wed - SFDC integration user got unauthorized/disconnected; (2) Thu - customer changed the SFDC integration user but the new user lacked the PSA managed package license, preventing outbound messages from SFDC to Momentum.
root_cause: Customer-side SFDC configuration issues. The integration user got unauthorized (Wed), and when the customer changed to a new integration user (Thu), that user was missing the PSA license, blocking visibility on PSA objects/fields needed for the Invite outbound message flow.
still_relevant: `mitigated` - Recommended backup service account with useForOrg, and suggested forcing SFDC login page during authorization (incognito window).

what_broke: Intermittent meeting processing failures affecting ~37 meetings across 12 customer organizations (including Ramp with 16 affected calls) over Oct 10-13. Summaries and signals were not generated.
root_cause: UNKNOWN (messages don't describe the specific technical root cause, only that the issue was resolved and would not manifest going forward; meetings were re-run successfully).
still_relevant: `mitigated` - Issue was resolved and meetings were re-run.

what_broke: Recall.ai bots were stuck in "joining_call" state and unable to join Google Meet calls. 4 calls across 2 orgs (Owner and Sardine) were initially affected, plus 1 additional (WALT Labs). A separate related issue from Recall caused a bot to fail uploading its recording (unrecoverable).
root_cause: Recall.ai (third-party bot provider) had an internal issue preventing a subset of Google Meet bots from joining calls. Recall rolled out a patch to fix the behavior.
still_relevant: `mitigated` - Recall patched the issue. Momentum was evaluating the Recall integration for reliability given repeated similar incidents.

what_broke: AWS global outage cascaded into LaunchDarkly failures (flag delivery service down), causing Momentum webapp inaccessibility, meeting processing delays, and broken consent screens. LaunchDarkly streaming connections failed, and Temporal worker pods ran out of memory (OOMKilled).
root_cause: AWS outage in us-east-1 caused LaunchDarkly's streaming service to go down. Momentum relied on LD streaming for feature flags, so when streaming broke, the app could not evaluate feature flags properly. Additionally, Temporal worker pods hit OOM (unrelated to the outage itself).
still_relevant: `mitigated` - Hotfix deployed to switch LD from streaming to polling mode. Action items: add LD relay proxy (useLdd) for local state in Redis, review Centrifugo resilience, address Temporal worker OOM, introduce multi-region support.

what_broke: Same AWS outage caused Recall.ai bot failures - bots failed to record meetings, failed to enable recording, and transcript processing failed with 404 errors from Recall API. Multiple orgs affected (Zscaler, Owner, Pendo, Confluent, etc.). Some meetings had bots present but recordings were lost.
root_cause: AWS outage disrupted Recall.ai services. When Momentum deployed multi-region failover for Recall, it introduced a bug where rescheduled meetings couldn't delete old bots from Recall's records (Axios not handling 404 status codes properly), causing RECALL_BOT_TRANSCRIPT_PROCESSING_FAILED status.
still_relevant: `mitigated` - Multi-region Recall failover deployed, affected meetings reprocessed, Axios error handling fix identified.

what_broke: Duplicate Slack dealroom/assist channels were created for multiple customers (notably Freshworks, Zscaler, and others). Additionally, ~2k Zscaler assist channels were incorrectly soft-deleted, and ~2k assist channels from other orgs were also affected. Summaries failed to post to some Slack channels due to missing channels.
root_cause: A gap in the channel import flow where private channels were treated as "deleted," causing the system to create new duplicate channels. When the dealroom creation workflow was briefly disabled (Thu-Fri), it compounded the issue. The soft-deletion of assist channels appears related to the same logic flaw.
still_relevant: `mitigated` - 1761 Zscaler assist channels undeleted, duplicate channels cleaned up manually. Root cause (private channels treated as deleted) identified but unclear if fully fixed for all orgs.

what_broke: Call dialer events (Outreach/Salesloft) were not being processed for any org using Call Intelligence (CI). ~1052 meetings across 6 orgs were affected (Chowly 183, Clipboard Health 635, Smart-Moving 2, Confluent 35, Iterable 1, Push Operations 196).
root_cause: Missing GCP IAM permission `iam.serviceAccounts.signBlob` on the production service account, preventing upload of recordings to GCP. This blocked Gladia transcription. The permission was needed for a new feature/service introduced in October but the corresponding k8s service account permissions were not updated.
still_relevant: `mitigated` - Permission added via infrastructure PR, all missed calls backfilled and reprocessed.

what_broke: Data Insights section of meeting summaries stopped posting to Slack threads. Affected 1400+ summaries across many orgs (Zscaler alone had 950 affected meetings). Summaries themselves still posted, but the Data Insights visual section was missing.
root_cause: A null/undefined insight value caused the Data Insights posting to fail silently. The code did not handle null/undefined insights properly.
still_relevant: `mitigated` - Fix deployed, ability to repost data insights added, unit tests for null/undefined insights to be added, log alert on failure created.

what_broke: Unable to upload call dialer recordings to GCP due to missing `iam.serviceAccounts.signBlob` permission. Affected 7 customers with ~666 meetings. The issue had been ongoing since Oct 17 but went undetected until Nov 14. All customers using any dialer with CI flag enabled were impacted.
root_cause: Same root cause as the Oct 27 incident - missing GCP IAM permission `iam.serviceAccounts.signBlob` on the production service account. The permission fix from October apparently did not fully resolve the issue or regressed.
still_relevant: `mitigated` - Permission re-added, all affected calls backfilled (including Oct 17-27 backlog of 90 calls for Chowly and Clipboard Health). Concern raised about lack of alerting - the error went undetected for nearly a month.

what_broke: Pinecone (vector database dependency) started returning 504 errors on read path operations in the us-east-1 region, affecting serverless operations.
root_cause: Pinecone infrastructure issue causing 5xx errors on read path operations. Resolved on Pinecone's side.
still_relevant: yes -- Pinecone is still used as a dependency; another outage could recur.

what_broke: All post-call analysis workflows (CallAiSignalFetch, CallSummaryNotification, ContactAutomation, SuggestEmail, etc.) were failing with "Cannot read properties of undefined" error, affecting ~50 customers with Call Intelligence enabled.
root_cause: A code bug involving implicit `this` binding in TypeScript -- a deployed change caused undefined property access in workflow actions. `noImplicitThis` tsconfig option would have caught it.
still_relevant: mitigated -- `noImplicitThis` TypeScript config was added via PR to prevent recurrence.

what_broke: Recall (meeting bot provider) delayed delivering meeting data and some meetings had missing transcripts. 133 meetings were initially not imported due to missing meeting URL data from Recall.
root_cause: Recall's system failed to provide meeting URL data for a subset of meetings, causing them to not be imported. Of 133 affected meetings, 48 were recoverable (41 recovered, 7 skipped due to no external attendees/short duration), 2 had permanent data loss due to expired media on Recall's side.
still_relevant: yes -- Recall is still used as a meeting bot provider; similar data delivery issues could recur.

what_broke: Scheduler pods stopped working for ~2.7 hours (starting ~6:47am PST), causing all cron-based scheduled tasks to be skipped including SYNC_*_CALLS schedulers and UPDATE_ZOOM_MEETING_SETTINGS. ~18 customers affected with some unrecoverable missed call recordings.
root_cause: A DB table was added to the PURGE_ORGANIZATION_DATA process that was excessively slow to delete data, saturating the connection pool of both scheduler pods. Orphaned DB connections (from force-killed scheduler pods) were found in locked state on the `assist_definition` table.
