still_relevant: mitigated -- DB query timeout (5 min max) was added, alert policy on waiting connections was created, and the problematic purge process was paused.

what_broke: Custom meeting summaries (MeetingSummaryCustomAiTask) were failing to be posted for multiple customer orgs (Owner, 1Password, Dealfront, Tebra, Veriff). Issue persisted for ~30 days before discovery, with 324 failed workflows.
root_cause: A shadow AI task version (MeetingSummaryCustomTaskV2) was deployed but had a className parsing error ("Failed to parse className MeetingSummaryCustomTaskV2"). The error was silently swallowed -- no error logs were raised to alert about failures.
still_relevant: mitigated -- bug fixed, failed workflows re-ran, and learnings documented about validating shadow AI task versions and fixing error swallowing in MeetingSummaryCustomAiTask code path.

what_broke: A global Cloudflare outage caused Zoom to go down, which prevented meeting bots from joining calls. 8 meetings (primarily Zscaler and Veriff) were not recorded and were non-recoverable.
root_cause: Global Cloudflare infrastructure outage affecting Zoom and other third-party providers. Recall-based meetings had less impact due to an existing "long scheduler" mechanism, but Zoom-based calls had no similar resilience mechanism.
still_relevant: mitigated -- long scheduler for Zoom-based calls was planned as a follow-up to reduce future impact from similar outages.

what_broke: Gladia (transcription provider) had silent realtime transcription failures on December 9th and 10th, causing call transcript imports to fail. Meetings ended up stuck in "no transcript" state.
root_cause: Gladia allows only a fixed number of concurrent transcription sessions. High volume caused the limit to be breached, and transcript generation failed silently -- no error was thrown, no alert triggered, transcripts simply never arrived. Neither Recall nor Gladia have reliable retry mechanisms.
still_relevant: mitigated -- an automatic transcript recovery fallback mechanism was implemented that detects meetings in problem states (NO_TRANSCRIPT, RECORDING_MISSING_EVERYONE_LEFT) and triggers async transcription jobs. All failures were recovered.

what_broke: Call summaries were not being posted to Slack for 5 organizations (orgs 174, 334, 336, 385, 387). ~200 meeting summaries failed to post. Workflows relying on `aiSettingSummaryId` were failing because the referenced records had been deleted.
root_cause: Race condition in UI: creating an AI Notification took a few seconds, allowing users to double-click the button. This created duplicate workflows. When users deleted the "extra" duplicate, the `aiSettingSummaryId` record was deleted via Hasura (DeleteAISettingSummary mutation), but the original workflow still referenced that now-deleted ID. The deletion flow via frontend/Hasura was non-transactional -- it deleted the ai_setting_summary without cleaning up referencing workflows.
still_relevant: mitigated -- button disabled during loading to prevent double-click, and AI Summary Notification deletion was made more resilient. Orphaned workflows were manually cleaned up.

what_broke: Outlook calendar events sync failed for 40 users across 7 customer orgs. Outlook events were not being synced from December 25 to January 5, resulting in 43 calls that failed to be recorded (non-recoverable).
root_cause: The Microsoft OAuth application secret had expired, causing 401 authentication failures when attempting to refresh tokens. This generated >30,000 errors/day starting Dec 25.
still_relevant: mitigated -- secret was renewed, but the underlying problem of secret expiry monitoring may still need proactive alerting.

what_broke: The call library page stopped rendering for all CI (Call Intelligence) customers, showing an error instead of call recordings. Frontend-only impact, no data loss.
root_cause: A Hasura config change renamed a GraphQL field from `name` to `fullName`, breaking frontend queries that still referenced the old field name. The Hasura config revert timed out during prod deployment, so a frontend fix (replacing `name` with `fullName` in GraphQL queries) was deployed instead.
still_relevant: mitigated -- fix was deployed via frontend PR. Incident report was requested to identify gaps in deployment process (Hasura schema changes not coordinated with frontend).

what_broke: Signals v2 transcoder event processing workflows got stuck in "Running" state. Temporal search queries failed because numeric query args were being wrapped in quotes incorrectly.
root_cause: A Temporal Cloud API change broke query syntax for numeric values (wrapping them in quotes caused search failures). The fix involved not quoting numeric Temporal query args. ~503 workflows got stuck and needed manual batch resets.
still_relevant: mitigated - fix deployed (PR #7608) and a resilience improvement (PR #7609) was made to target Temporal workflows directly instead of relying on Temporal search.

what_broke: Smart clips (signalProcessAllForMeeting workflows) failed because GCP Transcoder rejected resource labels containing uppercase characters in the workflow ID.
root_cause: A code change ("signals v2: more resilient transcoder event processing") passed the Temporal workflow ID as a GCP resource label, but GCP labels only allow lowercase characters. The workflow ID contained uppercase letters, causing INVALID_ARGUMENT errors. The change was reverted and ~384 failed workflows had to be batch-reset manually.
still_relevant: mitigated - the PR was reverted (PR #7628) and a hotfix deployed. The resilience improvement would need to lowercase or encode workflow IDs for GCP labels.

what_broke: Pinecone vector database deletions failed due to high latency, causing meeting processing workflows to fail. Primarily impacted one large customer (1Password) -- their recorded calls were not being transcribed for ~3 hours.
root_cause: A Pinecone query executor went offline due to a known bug in Pinecone's infrastructure. This caused a growing backlog of write/delete requests. Pinecone engineering restarted the executor to clear the backlog.
still_relevant: mitigated - Pinecone investigated the niche known bug. 15 failed workflows were reset after resolution.

what_broke: Users across multiple customers experienced "API Error" messages in the web app and could not log in or see full navigation. Already-logged-in users had their sessions broken.
root_cause: A frontend code change implementing silent token refresh (INF-247) did not handle the edge case of already-logged-in users. When JWT TTL was reduced from 10 hours to 1 hour (for pentesting/SOC2 remediation), the FE code failed to silently refresh tokens for existing sessions, causing widespread auth failures.
still_relevant: mitigated - the silent token refresh change was reverted (PR #6201, #6217, #6220), JWT TTL reverted to 10 hours for prod (infrastructure PR #1229). Planned to re-release under a feature flag with more testing.

what_broke: Cron scheduler workflows (executive_brief, scheduled_notification, scheduled_report) stopped running since 2026-01-30 00:30:00 UTC. Multiple orgs affected but impact was assessed as low since these are periodic report/notification workflows.
root_cause: A deployment introduced a bug that broke the PROCESS_WORKFLOW_CRONTABS functionality. The deployer acknowledged the error was not caught before deployment.
still_relevant: mitigated - fix deployed, some monthly/weekly workflows needed to be manually rerun.

what_broke: Hasura GraphQL validation failures occurred, causing API errors. Users experienced authentication and authorization issues in the web app.
root_cause: Related to the JWT TTL/refresh token changes (same root cause as the login-failures incident on Jan 28). The FE code changes and infrastructure TTL changes caused Hasura to receive expired/invalid tokens, triggering validation failures.
still_relevant: mitigated - resolved as part of the same revert of the refresh token changes and JWT TTL revert.

what_broke: Outlook calendar sync stopped working for the Smart Moving customer (org 314). Calls recorded in Zoom were not being ingested into Momentum since Jan 16.
root_cause: Outlook calendar sync required a user with `useForOrg` flag set to true. When that flag was removed for Smart Moving on Jan 16, the sync silently stopped. This was a pre-existing design limitation, not a recent regression.
still_relevant: mitigated - fix removed the need for `useForOrg` flag for Outlook sync. Datadog monitors added to alert if event sync stops for extended duration.

what_broke: Dialer call sync (Salesloft/Vonage/Outreach) stopped working since 2026-02-02 18:30:00 UTC. Meeting records were created but Gladia transcription was never initiated. 772 calls across 6 orgs affected.
root_cause: A code change broke the Meeting import logic for dialers. The change was reverted but deployment was delayed due to GitHub issues. Required emergency fast-track deployment.
still_relevant: mitigated - change reverted, fix deployed (PR #7904 to improve import logic), all 772 affected calls reprocessed via dialer backfill.

what_broke: AI call notification summaries failed for MS Teams customers (Aptean, LeahAI, Conexium). The `SuggestEmail` assist action failed with `DatabaseValidationFailedError: "id" cannot be null or undefined`.
root_cause: A code change (PR #7982) broke the ContextBuilder for MS Teams users. The builder used `slackUserId` to look up users, but for Teams customers this was null. The `assist.createdById` was not set for Teams users because `extractMomentumUserIdFromContext` returned null when the Slack user ID was absent.
still_relevant: mitigated - fix deployed, all affected assist actions for the 3 orgs were reprocessed by resetting autoRetryCount.

what_broke: Temporal worker pods ran out of memory, causing a massive backlog of ~95k+ workflows. This impacted call processing, notifications, signals, autopilot, calendar updates, and Salesforce flow processing across many customers. ~1,995 workflows ended up in non-deterministic state.
root_cause: The recent migration of Assist Engine workflows to Temporal overloaded the `main` queue. A single customer's (Owner, org 92) Lead Flow workflow generated 50k+ Salesforce flow events, which combined with all other workflow processing, exhausted pod memory (5GB). The Salesforce context loading for each workflow was not cached, adding heavy DB load.
still_relevant: yes - ongoing at time of incident. Mitigations applied: pod count increased 6->8, memory increased 5GB->7GB, problematic workflow disabled. Proposed long-term fixes: LRU caching for Salesforce metadata, separate Temporal queue for Salesforce flow workflows with concurrency limits, alerts on long-running Temporal jobs.
